summary: Validated new /admin (password-gated) feature end-to-end. Backend: 16/16 pytest pass covering public POST /api/contact (no auth), GET /api/contact auth gating (401 no-token, 401 wrong-token, 200 valid-token, sorted desc, no mongo _id leak), POST /api/admin/verify auth (401/401/200), DELETE /api/contact/{id} auth (401/401, 404 unknown, full create->delete->verify-removal round trip, second delete returns 404). Frontend: /admin gate renders, wrong password shows 'Wrong password' toast and stays on gate, correct password (y2T6Aa...YK) unlocks Dashboard, localStorage 'mo_admin_token' is set, all 3 filter tabs (All/Form/Booking) visible and clickable, search input accepts text, refresh button works, delete button removes lead (confirm dialog accepted), page reload preserves session, sign out returns to gate and clears localStorage. Regression: landing page POST /api/contact (both normal + [Booking pre-qualify]) still works without auth; browser-side GET /api/contact correctly returns 401.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 23: ADMIN_TOKEN defaults to empty string and require_admin already rejects empty tokens (hmac.compare_digest with empty ADMIN_TOKEN always fails). Good — fails closed if env missing.
server.py uses hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison — correct for a shared secret.

Admin.js line 308: Storing plaintext admin password in localStorage is acceptable for this lightweight shared-secret model per the spec, but document that it is world-readable by any JS on the origin (XSS risk). Not a bug, just a trade-off note.

Admin.js on reload calls POST /api/admin/verify to validate, then renders dashboard. If the backend verify fails for reasons other than 401 (e.g. 500, offline), it silently clears the token and sends user to gate — acceptable. The axios.catch currently treats every failure as invalid token; that is fine for this use case.

Dashboard filter relies on message prefix '[Booking pre-qualify]' — tight coupling between landing-page payload format and admin classifier. If the booking form ever changes its prefix, all bookings will fall into 'Form'. Consider persisting a `source` enum field on ContactSubmission.
server.py still has `logger` defined below its use (carried from previous iteration) — works due to module-level binding at request time but fragile. Pre-existing issue, not new.
seed_data_creation: Pytest creates TEST_-prefixed contact_submissions rows (Alex Pytest, Booking User, Listcheck-like, DeleteMe which is then removed). UI regression also creates TEST_PublicRegression, TEST_PublicBooking, and TEST_DeleteFromUI (latter is deleted by the test). All prefixed with TEST_ for easy cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_mo_studio_api.py -v for full backend regression (public contact + admin auth). Frontend admin route: /admin, password is in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. localStorage key: 'mo_admin_token'. All admin buttons have data-testid: admin-gate, admin-password-input, admin-login-btn, admin-refresh, admin-logout, admin-filter-{all|form|booking}, admin-search, admin-leads-list, admin-lead-{id}, admin-lead-delete-{id}, admin-lead-email-{id}. Delete triggers browser confirm() — accept via page.once('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()).
